license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7eqhjdw
title: DNaseI and flowcell clearing for increasing long read yields and multi-sample sequencing
author: John Tyson

steps_raw.instruction: Mix together 200 µL FB + 2 µL 100 millimolar (mM) CaCl 2 + 2 µL DNaseI (2U per ul) to form DNaseI flowcell digestion buffer
Add 200ul of DNaseI Digestion buffer to the flowcell through the sample port and close
Incubate for 20mins to 2hrs depending on sample, monitoring the Single Pore : Strand numbers reported in MinKNOW
Flush flowcell by adding 400ul of FB buffer through the Sample Port
Add 500ul of FB + Tether to the flowcell through the Sample Port
Close Sample Port and Incubate for 5 mins
Open Sample Port and remove any air. Open SpotOn port and prime by addition of a small volume of FB + Tether through the Sample Port.
Start MinKNOW and monitor Single Pore : Strand numbers
Open Sample Port and remove any air as usually done when first using a flowcell
Once strand pore count has dropped to desired level stop MinKNOW
Open Open Sample Port and remove any air
Add 75ul freshly prepared library (DNA + SQB + LLB (optional)) by spotting onto the SpotON port. Once library has enetered the flowcell close first the SpotON Port followed by the Sample Port.
Now ready to Start the sequencing run in MinKNOW
